description: O*NET Cognitive Abilities (1.A.1) with behavioral anchors

focus_sub_dimensions.3.1_verbal_abilities: 1A1a_verbal (Oral Comprehension, Written Comprehension, Oral Expression, Written Expression)
focus_sub_dimensions.3.2_reasoning_abilities: 1A1b_reasoning (Fluency, Originality, Problem Sensitivity, Deductive, Inductive, Ordering, Category Flexibility)

1.A.1.name: Cognitive Abilities
1.A.1.description: Abilities that influence the acquisition and application of knowledge in problem solving

1.A.1.a.name: Verbal Abilities
1.A.1.a.description: Abilities that influence the acquisition and application of verbal information in problem solving

1.A.1.a.1.name: Oral Comprehension
1.A.1.a.1.description: The ability to listen to and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentences.

1.A.1.a.2.name: Written Comprehension
1.A.1.a.2.description: The ability to read and understand information and ideas presented in writing.

1.A.1.a.3.name: Oral Expression
1.A.1.a.3.description: The ability to communicate information and ideas in speaking so others will understand.

1.A.1.a.4.name: Written Expression
1.A.1.a.4.description: The ability to communicate information and ideas in writing so others will understand.

1.A.1.b.name: Idea Generation and Reasoning Abilities
1.A.1.b.description: Abilities that influence the application and manipulation of information in problem solving

1.A.1.b.1.name: Fluency of Ideas
1.A.1.b.1.description: The ability to come up with a number of ideas about a topic (the number of ideas is important, not their quality, correctness, or creativity).

1.A.1.b.2.name: Originality
1.A.1.b.2.description: The ability to come up with unusual or clever ideas about a given topic or situation, or to develop creative ways to solve a problem.

1.A.1.b.3.name: Problem Sensitivity
1.A.1.b.3.description: The ability to tell when something is wrong or is likely to go wrong. It does not involve solving the problem, only recognizing that there is a problem.

1.A.1.b.4.name: Deductive Reasoning
1.A.1.b.4.description: The ability to apply general rules to specific problems to produce answers that make sense.

1.A.1.b.5.name: Inductive Reasoning
1.A.1.b.5.description: The ability to combine pieces of information to form general rules or conclusions (includes finding a relationship among seemingly unrelated events).

1.A.1.b.6.name: Information Ordering
1.A.1.b.6.description: The ability to arrange things or actions in a certain order or pattern according to a specific rule or set of rules (e.g., patterns of numbers, letters, words, pictures, mathematical operations).

1.A.1.b.7.name: Category Flexibility
1.A.1.b.7.description: The ability to generate or use different sets of rules for combining or grouping things in different ways.

1.A.1.c.name: Quantitative Abilities
1.A.1.c.description: Abilities that influence the solution of problems involving mathematical relationships

1.A.1.c.1.name: Mathematical Reasoning
1.A.1.c.1.description: The ability to choose the right mathematical methods or formulas to solve a problem.

1.A.1.c.2.name: Number Facility
1.A.1.c.2.description: The ability to add, subtract, multiply, or divide quickly and correctly.

1.A.1.d.name: Memory
1.A.1.d.description: Abilities related to the recall of available information

1.A.1.d.1.name: Memorization
1.A.1.d.1.description: The ability to remember information such as words, numbers, pictures, and procedures.

1.A.1.e.name: Perceptual Abilities
1.A.1.e.description: Abilities related to the acquisition and organization of visual information

1.A.1.e.1.name: Speed of Closure
1.A.1.e.1.description: The ability to quickly make sense of, combine, and organize information into meaningful patterns.

1.A.1.e.2.name: Flexibility of Closure
1.A.1.e.2.description: The ability to identify or detect a known pattern (a figure, object, word, or sound) that is hidden in other distracting material.

1.A.1.e.3.name: Perceptual Speed
1.A.1.e.3.description: The ability to quickly and accurately compare similarities and differences among sets of letters, numbers, objects, pictures, or patterns. The things to be compared may be presented at the same time or one after the other. This ability also includes comparing a presented object with a remembered object.

1.A.1.f.name: Spatial Abilities
1.A.1.f.description: Abilities related to the manipulation and organization of spatial information

1.A.1.f.1.name: Spatial Orientation
1.A.1.f.1.description: The ability to know your location in relation to the environment or to know where other objects are in relation to you.

1.A.1.f.2.name: Visualization
1.A.1.f.2.description: The ability to imagine how something will look after it is moved around or when its parts are moved or rearranged.

1.A.1.g.name: Attentiveness
1.A.1.g.description: Abilities related to application of attention

1.A.1.g.1.name: Selective Attention
1.A.1.g.1.description: The ability to concentrate on a task over a period of time without being distracted.

1.A.1.g.2.name: Time Sharing
1.A.1.g.2.description: The ability to shift back and forth between two or more activities or sources of information (such as speech, sounds, touch, or other sources).

1.A.1.a.1.name: Oral Comprehension
1.A.1.a.1.description: The ability to listen to and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentences.

anchors.description: Understand a television commercial
Understand a coach's oral instructions for a sport
Understand a lecture on advanced physics

1.A.1.a.2.name: Written Comprehension
1.A.1.a.2.description: The ability to read and understand information and ideas presented in writing.

anchors.description: Understand signs on the highway
Understand an apartment lease
Understand an instruction book on repairing Artificial Intelligence systems

1.A.1.a.3.name: Oral Expression
1.A.1.a.3.description: The ability to communicate information and ideas in speaking so others will understand.

anchors.description: Place an order at a restaurant drive-thru
Give instructions to a lost motorist
Explain advanced principles of genetics to college freshmen

1.A.1.a.4.name: Written Expression
1.A.1.a.4.description: The ability to communicate information and ideas in writing so others will understand.

anchors.description: Write a note to remind someone to take food out of the freezer
Write a job recommendation for a subordinate
Write an advanced economics textbook

1.A.1.b.1.name: Fluency of Ideas
1.A.1.b.1.description: The ability to come up with a number of ideas about a topic (the number of ideas is important, not their quality, correctness, or creativity).

anchors.description: Name four different uses for a screwdriver
Think of as many ideas as possible for the name of a new company
Identify as many strategies as possible for a company acquisition and merger

1.A.1.b.2.name: Originality
1.A.1.b.2.description: The ability to come up with unusual or clever ideas about a given topic or situation, or to develop creative ways to solve a problem.

anchors.description: Use a credit card to open a locked door
Redesign job tasks to be interesting for employees
Invent a new type of man-made fiber

1.A.1.b.3.name: Problem Sensitivity
1.A.1.b.3.description: The ability to tell when something is wrong or is likely to go wrong. It does not involve solving the problem, only recognizing that there is a problem.

anchors.description: Recognize that an unplugged lamp won't work
Notice from a subtle difference in a client's mood that something is bothering them
Recognize an illness at an early stage of a disease when there are only a few symptoms

1.A.1.b.4.name: Deductive Reasoning
1.A.1.b.4.description: The ability to apply general rules to specific problems to produce answers that make sense.

anchors.description: Know that a stalled car can coast downhill
Decide what factors to consider in selecting stocks
Design an aircraft wing using principles of aerodynamics

1.A.1.b.5.name: Inductive Reasoning
1.A.1.b.5.description: The ability to combine pieces of information to form general rules or conclusions (includes finding a relationship among seemingly unrelated events).

anchors.description: Decide what to wear based on the weather report
Determine the prime suspect based on crime scene evidence
Diagnose a disease using results of many different lab tests

1.A.1.b.6.name: Information Ordering
1.A.1.b.6.description: The ability to arrange things or actions in a certain order or pattern according to a specific rule or set of rules (e.g., patterns of numbers, letters, words, pictures, mathematical operations).

anchors.description: Put things in numerical order
Follow the correct steps to make change
Assemble a wind turbine

1.A.1.b.7.name: Category Flexibility
1.A.1.b.7.description: The ability to generate or use different sets of rules for combining or grouping things in different ways.

anchors.description: Sort paintbrushes based on length
Classify flowers according to size, color, and smell
Classify man-made fibers in terms of their characteristics, like strength or cost

1.A.1.c.1.name: Mathematical Reasoning
1.A.1.c.1.description: The ability to choose the right mathematical methods or formulas to solve a problem.

anchors.description: Determine how much 20 oranges will cost when they are priced at 5 for 4 dollars
Decide how to calculate profits to determine the amounts of yearly bonuses
Determine the mathematics required to simulate a space craft landing on the moon

1.A.1.c.2.name: Number Facility
1.A.1.c.2.description: The ability to add, subtract, multiply, or divide quickly and correctly.

anchors.description: Add 2 and 7
Calculate a discounted price
Compute the interest payment that should be generated from an investment

1.A.1.d.1.name: Memorization
1.A.1.d.1.description: The ability to remember information such as words, numbers, pictures, and procedures.

anchors.description: Remember the number on your bus to be sure you get back on the right one
Recite the first names of the five people you just met
Recite a 300-word speech after studying it for 15 minutes

1.A.1.e.1.name: Speed of Closure
1.A.1.e.1.description: The ability to quickly make sense of, combine, and organize information into meaningful patterns.

anchors.description: Recognize a song after hearing only the first few notes
Make sense of messy handwriting
Interpret patterns on weather radar to decide if the weather is changing

1.A.1.e.2.name: Flexibility of Closure
1.A.1.e.2.description: The ability to identify or detect a known pattern (a figure, object, word, or sound) that is hidden in other distracting material.

anchors.description: Detect that your phone is ringing while at a concert
Look for a golf ball in tall grass
Identify camouflaged animals from a moving vehicle while on a safari

1.A.1.e.3.name: Perceptual Speed
1.A.1.e.3.description: The ability to quickly and accurately compare similarities and differences among sets of letters, numbers, objects, pictures, or patterns. The things to be compared may be presented at the same time or one after the other. This ability also includes comparing a presented object with a remembered object.

anchors.description: Sort mail according to ZIP codes with no time pressure
Read five pressure indicators in 10 seconds to make sure each is within safe limits
Inspect electrical parts for defects as they pass on a fast-moving assembly line

1.A.1.f.1.name: Spatial Orientation
1.A.1.f.1.description: The ability to know your location in relation to the environment or to know where other objects are in relation to you.

anchors.description: Use the floor plan to locate a store in a mall
Find your way through a dark room without hitting anything
Navigate an ocean voyage using a compass and landmarks

1.A.1.f.2.name: Visualization
1.A.1.f.2.description: The ability to imagine how something will look after it is moved around or when its parts are moved or rearranged.

anchors.description: Imagine how to place paper in a printer so that the letterhead comes out on top
Follow a diagram to assemble a metal storage cabinet
Anticipate future moves in a chess game

1.A.1.g.1.name: Selective Attention
1.A.1.g.1.description: The ability to concentrate on a task over a period of time without being distracted.

anchors.description: Answer a business call with coworkers talking nearby
Watch security monitors for intruders throughout the night shift
Study a technical manual while listening to loud construction sounds

1.A.1.g.2.name: Time Sharing
1.A.1.g.2.description: The ability to shift back and forth between two or more activities or sources of information (such as speech, sounds, touch, or other sources).

anchors.description: Listen to music while entering numbers into a spreadsheet
Watch street signs while driving at 30 miles an hour
Monitor radar and radio transmissions to keep track of heavy aircraft traffic
